contract: Use a simple border, not a decorative left accent bar.
Use a neutral surface over the neutral page background.
Use a filled page icon with white icon color.
Expose snapshot and mutation boundary metadata when available.
State must be readable as text and never depend on color alone.
Badge shape, height, padding, and icon spacing are generated from the design-system source.
Permission questions use allowed, prohibited, or not-applicable wording.
Execution timing questions use prohibited, ask each time, or auto wording.
Show the practical question before technical evidence.
Shared card padding and internal gaps are generated from the design-system source.
Do not nest decorative cards inside decorative cards.
Keep raw technical values secondary and copyable.
Selected menu icons use a filled category color with a white icon.
Unselected menu icons use a soft category color with a darker icon from the same category.
Disabled or unavailable menu options keep readable text and must not look selected.
Selection state must also be exposed through text, check mark, and aria-pressed.
Use this component for short decision-progress summaries such as local checks, CI stages, sync stages, or safety review groups.
Each row shows a plain-language label and a progress fraction on one line.
The label and progress fraction use the same size and weight as other overview-card primary values.
The progress fraction uses tabular numerals so the user can scan completion quickly.
Overall pass, review, stale, or blocked state stays in the card status chip or detail surface instead of repeating on every row.
Rows must preserve scanability across tables, settings, evidence, and activity lists.
Shared row padding and internal gaps are generated from the design-system source.
Row density changes must not change execution authority, evidence meaning, or workflow state.
Support mouse, keyboard, Escape close, focus return, and screen-reader labeling.
Contain long explanations that do not fit in short role tooltips.
Keep command and source details display-only.
Visible field bodies may show raw paths, commands, or IDs.
Tooltips stay short and explain the role of the value.
Copy controls identify the raw value being copied.
File/path role tooltips and copy-button popups have separate display-condition settings.
Tooltip bubbles are visible only while the pointer is over the trigger.
Copy feedback appears above the copy control and hides when the pointer leaves.
Technical values and copy controls stay on one row with the design-system affordance gap.
Command previews are display-only and non-executable.
They must not look like execution controls.
They show intent, risk, command text, and copy affordance.
Interactive controls must be keyboard reachable and show a visible focus ring.
Shared control dimensions are generated from the design-system source.
Semantic colors, disabled behavior, and execution authority remain owned by the feature layer.
Buttons that look actionable must be real buttons or links.
Design Studio edits design-system source values through safe visual controls.
The visual preview must show atom, molecule, and organism examples.
Upstream token changes must affect downstream preview levels without page-local CSS exceptions.
External product targets are product-local design-system sources, not Dashboard Control Center source files.
Show proposal orchestration as a control-plane workflow, not as an apply shortcut.
Mark AI, mock, template, and external product data as proposal-only until explicit approval and owner-tool verification.
Expose provider modes and target adapters without exposing credentials or arbitrary command execution.
External product targets remain plan-only until a separate product-local mutation contract is approved.
